rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the intended target component or implementation scope
Preserve the AADL architecture by maintaining the original component structure and connections
Use only visible identifiers that are explicitly provided in the current visible identifiers list
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in scopes where they would not be valid according to the AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax and visible identifiers in the generated annex blocks
Follow the pattern of placing assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
anti_patterns: Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes when they reference subcomponent ports
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in scopes where they would not be valid according to the AADL model
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
